pattern: Cross-multiplying the equality into a ratio shows the outer pair and the inner pair just need to share the same reduced fraction, so sweeping the array while matching left-side and right-side ratio tallies turns a four-index search into a running frequency count.
hints: nums[p]*nums[r] == nums[q]*nums[s] is the same statement as nums[p]/nums[q] == nums[s]/nums[r], so express both sides as a fraction reduced by their gcd and look for matches.

For a fixed pair (q, r) with a gap between them, tally every earlier compatible p by its reduced fraction against q, and every later compatible s by its reduced fraction against r, then multiply matching tallies.

Sweep r left to right; each time r advances, fold the newly-eligible (p, q) fraction tallies (for q = r-2's own left side) into a global map, and separately maintain, for the current r, a running suffix tally of (s, r) fractions accumulated from the right so the two sides can be multiplied together per (q, r).

edge_inputs.description: minimum length with no valid quadruple
all equal values, every spaced quadruple qualifies
values present but gaps too tight to ever qualify
distinct primes with one matching quadruple by ratio
all ones, maximum value range at the low end

stress.description: random moderate array with a mix of small repeated values to create real matches
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(7, min(size, 1000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 12) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
stress.note: small value range keeps genuine ratio matches common
